Off Calendar Dec-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. GABRIEL N. RUBIN, Esq. and YUKI CRUSE, Esq. of Jackson Lewis P.C. for Plaintiff Macy'S Inc.;
Atty. MATTHEW J. GAUGER, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (Tel: 916-443-6600) for Defendant Stationary Engineers, Local 39.

FURTHER STATUS CONFERENCE

(Hearing advanced from 1:30 p.m. to 9:30 a.m.)

The Court and counsel discuss the plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction.

The parties having indicated no change in circumstances since 12/4/20 in terms of strike activity, the Court does not set a hearing on plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction. The Court indicates that should circumstances change, either side may return to Court by way of an ex parte application.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Off Calendar

Status Conference
Off Calendar Dec-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. GABRIEL N. RUBIN, Esq. and YUKI CRUSE, Esq. of Jackson Lewis P.C. for Plaintiff Macy'S Inc.;
Atty. MATTHEW J. GAUGER, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (Tel: 916-443-6600) for Defendant Stationary Engineers, Local 39.

Status Conference

The Court receives an update from defense counsel that there has been an unconditional offer to return to work and that a cessation of picketing will be happening promptly.

The Court continues the hearings on plaintiff's motion for leave to amend and defendant's anti-SLAPP motion to 12/18/20 at 1:30 p.m.

The Court continues the evidentiary hearing on plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction to 12/30/20 at 1:30 p.m.

The Court instructs defense counsel to file a declaration by 12/7/20 to apprise the Court of the status of the picketing activity and whether activity has ceased or not. If picketing activity has not yet ceased, plaintiff's counsel may file their own declaration on the matter .

The Court indicates that if plaintiff wishes to request an advancement of the evidentiary hearing on plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction, plaintiff should do so by way of an ex parte application.

The Court defers discussing the logistics of the evidentiary hearing at this time (e.g. witnesses, exhibits, the presentation of evidence, etc.).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
